characters.name: Adam
characters.description: AdamBiographical InformationAliasGrant BishopAppearancesFirst"not slow not bad"Last"Stay Alive, Jessica Hyde"Portrayed byBrady Dustin HarrisAdamis a young boy sourced bythe HarvestfromHomeas a doppelganger forGrant Bishop. He is used to frame Grant for a Mass-Shooting in an attempt to flush him out and recover the manuscript for the comicUtopia. After Grant Bishop steals the manuscript forUtopia, he becomes a target for the Harvest, the members of which are determined to recover the comic and kill everyone who has seen any part of it. After he escapes with the manuscript from the convention center shown in"Life Begins", they have no idea where he is, but are able to recover a sample of his fingerprints from a door handle. They therefore decide to come up with a plan to get as many people as possible looking for him.Thomas Christiehosts a brainstorming meeting and one of the members of the Harvest suggests staging a kidnapping and putting out an AMBER alert. Christie describes this as being "very local news" and suggests instead that they stage a Mass-Shooting.Cara Frostfieldis against the idea, but everyone else present agrees that it's good. In order to implement the plan,Arby, an assassin with the Harvest, pulls Adam out one of his classes at Home. He has him wear a special pair of gloves with have been modified with Grant's fingerprints and has him touch the rifle that will be used for the shooting. He then has him enter the house of the shooting, making sure to be caught by the various security cameras and set the rifle on a table and touch one of the windows before exiting the house. Arby then enters the house, claims the rifle and murders the entire family, recognized by Cara Frostfield in the process. Afterwards, just as The Harvest planned, the story of the "Grant Bishop" mass murder is one of the top stories on the every news network, along with that of theStearns Fluepidemic.

characters.name: Alice Ward
characters.description: Alice Ward / AliceUS SeriesUK SeriesAppearancesFirstSeries 1, Episode 2(UK)LastSeries 2, Episode 6(UK)Portrayed byEmilia Jones(UK)Farrah Mackenzie(US)Alice Wardis a central character in both versions ofUtopia. Befriended byGrant, later entangled in the chase forThe Manuscript. In the Amazon Prime remake, she is only given the name ofAlice. Grant, whilst fleeingThe Network, breaks into a private school and sits underneath a tree, readingThe Manuscript. Alice approaches him, initially polite yet questioning, but Grant responds with hostility, so she responds with mutual curtness. She gives Grant the nickname of "twat" as she readsthe manuscriptover his shoulder. When she runs to return to her lessons, Grant begs for her not to leave, so she tells him, "Same time tomorrow, twat."

characters.name: Anya
characters.description: AnyaProfessional InformationOccupationNetworkmoleAppearancesFirstSeries 1, Episode 1(UK)LastSeries 1, Episode 6(UK)Portrayed byAnna Madeley(UK) "You speak again and I will fire this into your fucking face! Understand?" Anyais the Russian sex worker with whomMichael Dugdalehas an extramarital affair. She becomes pregnant with his child and her Russian Mafia bosses blackmail him with the knowledge of the affair and resulting fetus.

characters.name: Arby
characters.description: "Where is Jessica Hyde?"ArbyBiographical InformationBorn1970sAliasPietre (UK), John (US)Familial InformationSiblingsJessica Hyde(sister)ParentsPhilip Carvel(father)Brosca Carvel(mother)OthersTess(partner)AmandaProfessional InformationOccupationHitmanPlumberAppearancesFirstSeries 1, Episode 1(UK)LastSeries 2, Episode 6(UK)Portrayed byNeil Maskell(UK)Christopher Denham(US)Pietre Carvel, also known asR.BorArbyis the secondary antagonist turned protagonist ofUtopia. He is the emotionally nullified hitman associated withThe Networkwho seeks to kill those attempting to prevent the group's plan of achieving a reduced ecosystem via genocide and mass poisoning. He is the brother ofJessica Hyde, whom he obsesses over as she is his only remaining trusted family. "I think a child needs love." Pietre Carvel, also known asArbyis a main character in series 1 and 2 of Utopia. The firstborn son ofPhilip CarvelandBrosca, Arby was neglected by his father from a young age and raised for most of his life byThe Networkwith no idea of his family background. During series 2, Arby's leitmotif is Brainwave Playground.

characters.name: Artemis
characters.description: ArtemisAppearancesFirst"Tuesday's Child"Last"Tuesday's Child"Portrayed byCamryn ManheimArtemisis a character on the Amazon remake ofUtopia. In the mythos of theDystopiacomic, she isJessica Hyde's sworn protector. She is portrayed byCamryn Manheim. She tells Jessica Hyde thather fatheris dead, his mind ravaged by one of his own viral creations and then killed byMr. Rabbit. She taught Jessica how to fight. She placed Jessica's father in an asylum for his own safety. Jessica asks where Mr. Rabbit is and she tells her that she's atHome. She asks where Home is and she replies that it's not where it used to be and that the location can be found in the pages ofUtopia. Jessica tells her that she cracked easily and she explains that she's tired and ready to pack it in. She thinks it likely thatthe Harvestwill find her soon and, if they do, she'll crack just easily under the pressure from them. As such, Jessica has to kill her. She agrees to it, but warns her that she will resist reflexively before giving in. Jessica chokes her to death and then carves out her fingernails, revealing tattoos with numbers underneath each of them, a clue.

characters.name: Becky/Becky Todd
characters.description: Becky / Becky ToddUK SeriesUS SeriesProfessional InformationOccupationPostgrad studentAppearancesFirstSeries 1 Episode 1(UK)LastSeries 2 Episode 6(UK)Portrayed byAlexandra Roach(UK)Ashleigh LaThrop(US) Can I actually say what I want to say!? Beckyis a passionate and forthright woman searching for theUtopia manuscript. She believes that a conspiracy surrounds a degenerative genetic disease she carries, calledDeel's Syndrome, and that it has something to do with The Utopia Experiments.

characters.name: Bejan Chervo
characters.description: Bejan ChervoBiographical InformationAliasleszek kluszekProfessional InformationOccupationOriginal owner of The ManuscriptAppearancesOnlySeries 1, Episode 1(UK)Portrayed byMark Stobbart(UK) "Original manuscript, original drawings." Bejan Chervo, a wealthy man, purchasedThe Utopia ExperimentsPart Two from Doomsday Comics.Bejan, a regular visitor on The Utopia Experiment Forum, had intentions of meeting up withBecky,Ian,WilsonandGrantto show themThe Manuscript, but was found by Lee and Arby and pushed off of his balcony. His death was made to look like suicide and his medical records were altered to show he had depression, which was false, as discovered byWilson.

characters.name: Betsy Ruskin
characters.description: Betsy RuskinProfessional InformationOccupationU.S. Army PrivateWorkplaceSt. Louis Hotzoneentrance(stationed)AppearancesFirst"Tuesday's Child"Last"Order 2472"Portrayed byDaryn Whitney HarrellPrivate Betsy Ruskinis a character on Prime Video'sUtopia. She is a private with the U.S. Army. She is portrayed byDaryn Whitney Harrell. Betsy Ruskin stands outside the checkpoint into theSt. Louis Hotzone, guarding it. WhenMichael Stearnswants to enter the hotzone, he speaks with her and she tells him that nobody is allowed inside without authorization. He claims to have authorization from the head of the CDC herself. He challenges her to call her and waste the time of an important person, thinking that he can bluff his way in. She, however, replies that she'll do just that and he is forced to beat a hasty retreat. Later, when Stearns is back as part of a group of protesters, she calls his name, telling him that he's been granted clearance. He steps forward, telling her that he won't enter without his "assistant,"Dale Warwick. She agrees to it, telling him it's okay so long as Warwick puts on a biosuit as well.

characters.name: Bev
characters.description: BevProfessional InformationOccupationDepartment of Health workerAppearancesFirstSeries 1, Episode 1(UK)LastSeries 1, Episode 3(UK)Portrayed byEleanor Matsuura(UK)BevisMichael Dugdale's colleague at the Department of Health. She is friendly ofMichaelbut also can become disapproving, as shown when she scoldedMichael's decision to order the Russian flu vaccine.

characters.name: Brian
characters.description: BrianProfessional InformationOccupationResearcher, scientistWorkplaceChicago City CollegeAppearancesFirst"Just a Fanboy"Last"Just a Fanboy"Portrayed bySean Bradley Brianis a character on Prime Video'sUtopia. He is a researcher and scientist atChicago City College. He is portrayed bySean Bradley. Michael Stearnsspots Brian walking down a hallway of Chicago City College by his basement lab. He tries to stop him, asking if there's been any word on the funding for his sequencer, as his research is limited without it. He starts to tell him he feels like he's being systematically prevented from doing his work. Brian cuts him off, telling him that there's no plot against him, that the dean simply doesn't think about him at all and there's nothing in the budget for him. He asks why not. "Lot of viruses out there," Brian replies and Stearns shoots back that hediscovered one. Brian replies that his research was worthwhile when they thought the virus he discovered was a threat, but that was seven years ago. He tells Stearns to be grateful that the dean has forgotten him and walks away.

characters.name: Brosca Carvel
characters.description: Brosca CarvelFamilial InformationMaritalPhilip Carvel(husband)ChildrenArby(son)Jessica Hyde(daughter)Professional InformationOccupationHousewife(presumed)AppearancesOnlySeries 2, Episode 1(UK)Portrayed byAnca-Ioana Androne(UK) "What have you done to my Pietre?" Brosca Carvelwas a character who appears in series 2 ofUtopiaduring the seventies flashback episode. The wife ofPhilip Carveland mother ofArbyandJessica Hyde, Brosca suspected that Philip had done something to Arby and after having him arrested for it, died while giving birth to Jessica although it is heavily implyed that her death was actually caused byThe Network.

characters.name: Brosca Carvel

characters.name: Cara Frostfield
characters.description: Cara FrostfieldFamilial InformationMaritalMarriedProfessional InformationOccupationAgent ofthe HarvestAppearancesFirst"Just a Fanboy"Last"not slow not bad"Portrayed byFiona DourifCara Frostfieldis a character on Prime Video'sUtopiaand an agent ofthe Harvest. She is portrayed byFiona Dourif. Cara Frostfield has been a long-time associate ofKevin Christie. Christie has known her since she interned for him while attending college and he attended her wedding and the christenings of her children. Frostfield attends a meeting of the Harvest led Kevin Christie's son,Thomas Christie. She tells Thomas thatMichael Stearnswas supposed to sound the victory bell for their vaccine for theStearns Flu. Thomas agrees, but says that it didn't work out that way. The subject of discussion then turns toGrant Bishop, a young boy who possesses the manuscript ofUtopia, a comic that has within its pages the secrets of their plot, which could be used to derail their plans. Thomas tells the group to come up with a way to flush him out, to make it so that everyone in America is looking for him. They come up with a plot to frame Grant for a mass murder, but Cara objects to this as being too extreme, asking if nobody else in the room objects to the murder of an innocent family at a park. At this, Thomas comments that he understands about her being a mother, but she replies that it has nothing to do with being weak or sentimental, that there are simply some lines which can't be crossed. He asks her if she's having doubts about their larger purpose and tells her to "lean in."

He asks her if she's having doubts about their larger purpose and tells her to "lean in."

characters.name: Charlotte
characters.description: CharlotteFamilial InformationSiblingsLilyParentsDale Warwick(claimed)AppearancesFirst"not slow not bad"Last"Order 2472"Portrayed byHadley RobinsonCharlotteis a character on Prime Video'sUtopia. She is a teenage girl who is raised to fulfill a purpose bythe Harvest, part of a plot known asthe Undoing. She is the twin ofLilyand after she is sacrificed, Lily takes on her identity. Charlotte is brought to theSt. Louis Hotzonefor theStearns Fluand placed on a bed inside of a tent, a supposed sufferer of the flu.Dale Warwick, a man posing as her father, then enters withMichael Stearns, a scientist who has discovered a formula that apparently serves as both a vaccine and a cure for the flu. He tells Stearns that his daughter is a fighter and strong, pleading with him to help her. After asking a series of basic questions about Charlotte's medical history, he agrees to test his cure on her. After Stearns leaves the tent, another girl enters who looks just like Charlotte. Warwick tells the two that he has something for them, a "last meal." The two gorge on delicious food, much of which they have never had or even seen before.Dr. Kevin Christiethen enters the tent, calling them "sweethearts," and they hug him. He asks if they've decided and Lily says that they wanted him to pick. He seems unsure about this at first, but points back and forth between them, reciting a rhyme, before landing on Charlotte. This means that Lily is to take her place and pretend to recover from the "flu," while Charlotte is taken away in a bodybag. Christie inserts a disc with a barcode into her mouth, asking her a question he often asks: "What have you done today to earn your place in this crowded world?" She smiles broadly.

characters.name: Christian Donaldson
characters.description: Christian DonaldsonSeries 1 (UK)Series 2 (UK)Biographical InformationDied2014 - Shot in the face byLeeProfessional InformationOccupationScientistAppearancesFirstSeries 1, Episode 3(UK)LastSeries 2, Episode 4(UK)Portrayed bySimon McBurney(UK, series 1)Michael Maloney(UK, series 2) "Are you having a heart attack? ARE YOU HAVING A FUCKING HEART ATTACK?" Christian Donaldsonwas a recurring character in series 1 and 2 of Utopia. A formerly distinguished scientist, Donaldson's career was ruined byThe Networkwhen he discovered something he shouldn't have about the SARS outbreak. Donaldson was recast in series 2 for unknown reasons.

Almost nothing is known about Christos,Jessica Hydeknowing him best after spending many years on the run with him and even then knowing little about him.

characters.name: Colleen
characters.description: ColleenFamilial InformationMaritalMichael Stearns(husband)Professional InformationOccupationMortgage brokerHarvest sleeper agentAppearancesFirst"Just a Fanboy"Last"Stay Alive, Jessica Hyde"Portrayed byJeanine Serralles(US) Colleenis one of the characters in the American version of television seriesUtopia. She is portrayed byJeanine Serralles. Colleen is a mortgage broker who is married toMichael Stearns, having found him later in life. She encourages him to step forward and assert himself as a scientist in the midst of a national health crisis. Colleen is her husband’s biggest cheerleader and confidence booster.[1]Colleen is secretly an agent ofthe Harvestand is forced to activate when Stearns gets too close to uncovering the truth about the conspiracy. Michael is shocked, as Colleen played her role perfectly, and he had no idea that she was anything other than the loving wife that she acted as.

characters.name: Conran Letts
characters.description: Conran LettsProfessional InformationOccupationCEOAppearancesFirstSeries 1, Episode 1(UK)LastSeries 1, Episode 5(UK)Portrayed byStephen Rea(UK) "I've done so many terrible things. But what we are doing is right." Conran Lettswas the CEO ofCorvadtLtd., a pharmaceutical mega-corporation based in London that specializes in vaccine development and epidemiology. He was aligned withThe Network. Though Letts appeared to be the one giving orders, he was actually a figurehead under the control of hisassistant.

characters.name: Conran Letts
characters.description: Conran LettsProfessional InformationOccupationCEOAppearancesFirstSeries 1, Episode 1(UK)LastSeries 1, Episode 5(UK)Portrayed byStephen Rea(UK) "I've done so many terrible things. But what we are doing is right." Conran Lettswas the CEO ofCorvadtLtd., a pharmaceutical mega-corporation based in London that specializes in vaccine development and epidemiology. He was aligned withThe Network. Though Letts appeared to be the one giving orders, he was actually a figurehead under the control of hisassistant.

characters.name: Dale Warwick
characters.description: Dale WarwickFamilial InformationChildrenCharlotte(claimed)Professional InformationOccupationAgent ofthe HarvestAppearancesPortrayed byTim Hopper Dale Warwickis a character on Prime Video'sUtopia. He claims to be a scientist knowledgeable in virology who graduated from Stanford and is the father of a teenage girl namedCharlotte. In actuality, he is an agent ofthe Harvest, a shadowy organization headed byDr. Kevin Christie. He is portrayed byTim Hopper. In St. Louis, an epicenter for anoutbreakof theStearns Flu, Dale Warwick visits a cafe and meetsMichael Stearns, who has been talking with some protesters inside the cafe. Stearns is a scientist who has developed a vaccine and cure for the flu and who is seeking entry into the hotzone. Warwick tells Stearns that his daughterCharlotteis inside the hotzone, but he has no idea whether or not she's infected. The two return to the entrance to the hotzone and protest together outside, until an Army private informs Stearns that he has been granted access. Stearns then comments that he won't go in without his "partner," Warwick, and the private agrees to admit Warwick as well.

characters.name: Ethan Lander
characters.description: Ethan LanderFamilial InformationMaritalOlivia(Fiancee)AppearancesFirst"Life Begins"Last"Life Begins"Portrayed byCalum WorthyEthan Landeris a character on Prime Video'sUtopia. He is implied to be engaged toOlivia, though the two have not yet actually married. Their lives take a dramatic turn when Olivia is left a house by her grandfather and Ethan discovers the manuscript for a comic namedUtopiawithin it. Ethan and Olivia pull up outside of the home, excited to be starting their new life together. They embrace excitedly, but upon entering discover that while the house looks great on the outside, it is a real mess on the inside. With the house filled with years of accumulated clutter and debris, the two get down to work on some serious cleaning. As they do so, Ethan discovers an unusual comic with grisly images, titled asUtopiaand said to be the conclusion toDystopia. Olivia wants him to just throw it out and keep cleaning, but he tells her that it could be worth something, that last year a man sold an oldBatmancomic he found in his attic for around a million dollars. He does a quick search on the Internet and finds many results about theDystopiacomic, telling Olivia that fanboys are going to go nuts over the comic. He posts online that the comic will be sold at a convention calledFringecon, quickly attracting the attention of a select group of fans. He and Olivia set up a private room atFringecon, where they see bidders for the comic individually, taking down their bids on a sheet of paper. He allows each bidder a look at the cover of the comic and a single page. As the bidding draws towards a close and Olivia talks with a friend about who to go back to, Ethan meets with a man namedPhillip Carsonwho tells him that he'd like to preempt all bidding and walk out with the comic then and there. Ethan tells him to put down his information, but he pulls out a briefcase and from it, $20,000 in cash. "How's that?" he asks. Stunned, Ethan eyes Olivia, who smiles widely. Later, Ethan and Olivia party it up with their friends, celebrating their big sale, when there is a knock at their door. Two men,ArbyandRod, enter, demanding to know who they sold the comic to and who else has seen it. He tells them that it's a private party and they pull out a gun, demanding answers. Olivia gives them the list of bidders and then they pull out a case with syringes filled with deadly poison, but tell them that it's a robbery and that the shots will simply put them to sleep for a bit. Before the two of them are injected, Olivia admits to Ethan that she's scared, but he tries to reassure her, telling her that they wouldn't swab their arms if they were going to hurt them. Before collapsing, he tells her that he loves her.

characters.name: Geoff Lawson
characters.description: Geoff LawsonProfessional InformationOccupationMinister for HealthAppearancesFirstSeries 1, Episode 1(UK)LastSeries 2, Episode 6(UK)Portrayed byAlistair Petrie(UK) "What is it they say? Mission complete." Geoff Lawsonwas a recurring character during series 1 and 2 of the BritishUtopia. Geoff became Minister for Health afterMichael Dugdaletricked Greg Ashby into signing for the Russian flu vaccine, leading to his resignation. After thanking Dugdale for his support, he made it known he worked forThe Network.

characters.name: Grant Leetham
characters.description: Grant Leetham / Grant BishopUK SeriesUS SeriesAppearancesFirstSeries 1, Episode 1(UK)Portrayed byOliver Wollford(UK)Javon Walton(US)Grantis a character in both theoriginal British versionand Prime VideoremakeofUtopia. In both versions, he is a young boy who has had a tough life and becomes embroiled in a conspiracy related to the manuscript of an unusual comic. On the Channel 4 version, he is portrayed byOliver Wollford.Javon Walton, credited as Javon "Wanna" Walton, assumes the role in the Prime Video version. "Yeah, I've got ambitions." Troubled and practically feralGrant Leethamhas built up a wall of bravado while his mother, on the other hand, can't cope and has succumbed to the numbness of booze. Grant pretends online to be a city trader who drives a Porsche and shags supermodels, but he is actually just eleven years old. Grant plays as a supporting but important character in the series for he protected the manuscript despite of being in hard circumstances.

characters.name: Greg Ashby
characters.description: Greg AshbyProfessional InformationOccupationMinister for Health(resigned)AppearancesOnlySeries 1, Episode 1(UK)Portrayed byOliver Dimsdale(UK)Greg Ashbyis a character in the British television seriesUtopia.

characters.name: Hailey Alvez
characters.description: Hailey AlvezAppearancesFirst"Just a Fanboy"Last"Talking Hurts"Portrayed byFelisha TerrellHailey Alvezis a character on Prime Video'sUtopia. She is portrayed byFelisha Terrell. Hailey Alvez is a reporter for the news network BSN News.Kevin Christieagrees to an interview with her about his newSimproproduct, a synthetic meat substitute. He expects the interview to go well, his sonThomas Christiehaving vetted her and discovered that she did a favorable peace about his measles vaccination in Niger the previous year. The interview turns out, however, to be a disaster for Christie, as Alvez accuses him of using poor children as a means to test his product and even suggests that it may be responsible for adeadly fluoutbreak. This aggravates Christie, who isn't used to having things not go his way. Alvez later interviews a woman, "Emily Nowak," about the death ofDale Warwick, her supposed co-worker. Unknown to Alvez, Nowak is actually a creation ofthe Harvest, sent to put out a false life narrative regarding Warwick, who was also one of their agents prior to committing suicide.

He died in a pre-meditated collision with a truck. His character is in the British television seriesUtopia.

characters.name: Jen Dugdale
characters.description: Jen DugdaleFamilial InformationMaritalMichael Dugdale(husband)Professional InformationOccupationScientistAppearancesFirstSeries 1, Episode 1(UK)Portrayed byRuth Gemmell(UK)Jen DugdaleisMichael Dugdale's wife. She and her husband desperately want a child but are having trouble conceiving despite attempts at IVF.
